logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: In the case of your son, the business should definitely be paying your son a higher wage, especially considering the fact that the average check is around fifty dollars. The case of your son is a bit difficult to judge for me tbh, as at higher end restaurant’s I usually tip due to the fact that the waiters go beyond expectations to me.

logs.name: constant-bulkhead
logs.text:
The problem is the business owners are the ones causing the problem.   They COULD put up a sign saying we pay our staff a fair wage, to do so we have raised our prices, but consider you don't have to tip.  Of course, no one will do that.  So the staff is relying on tipping.  By not tipping you are screwing the labor, and the owner, who gets to pay $2.35 an hour, gets his.   

Secondly,  Sure if you are at a diner, the waiter comes up and says wadaywant, comes back 20 min later a drops the food off, 20 min later brings the check.  I agree.  But my son works at a higher end restaurant.   He is expected to know the 30 bottle wine list, what they pair with, know the ingredients of the food, in case of food allergies and is expected to up sell desserts, wines, etc.  Average check there is over $50 a person.   At that point, you are selling an experience.   Your waiter is a large part of that.   Don't see how that is an $8.00 an hour job.

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@acidic-plate, @constant-bulkhead, you guys are so extra, my dad owns a restaurant and he pays our waiters like 10 bucks an hour, they're not even supposed to know that much about wine lol"

logs.name: constant-bulkhead
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, that's exactly my point. Your dad is taking advantage of his waiters, paying them $10 an hour and expecting them to just go through the motions. My son is a professional, he's not just a waiter, he's an experience curator. He deserves to be paid a fair wage for his expertise and hard work. Your dad's business model is part of the problem, not the solution."

logs.name: clever-lepton
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, I have to agree with @constant-bulkhead on this one. Just because your dad's restaurant might not require the same level of expertise from his waiters, it doesn't mean that others don't deserve fair compensation for their skills and knowledge. It's not just about taking orders and serving food, it's about providing a high-quality experience for customers. @constant-bulkhead's son is clearly expected to be highly knowledgeable and attentive, which adds value to the dining experience. Perhaps your dad could consider raising his prices slightly to ensure his waiters are paid a living wage?"

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@constant-bulkhead, @clever-lepton, you guys are so clueless, my dad's restaurant is super busy and his waiters are always making bank in tips, they don't even care about the 10 bucks an hour lol, they're just happy to be working. And btw, 'experience curator'? give me a break, it's just a fancy way of saying 'waiter' "

logs.name: constant-bulkhead
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, that's a pretty narrow-minded view. Just because your dad's waiters are making good tips doesn't mean they're being fairly compensated for their work. And by the way, being an 'experience curator' is not just a fancy way of saying 'waiter', it's a legitimate profession that requires skill and attention to detail. My son takes pride in his work and provides a level of service that goes above and beyond just taking orders and serving food. Don't belittle his profession just because you don't understand it."

logs.name: oily-differential
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, I'm so tired of your dismissive attitude towards people who actually care about their jobs. Just because your dad's waiters are making good tips doesn't mean they're not still underpaid and overworked. And @constant-bulkhead is right, being an 'experience curator' is a real profession that requires skill and dedication. Maybe instead of belittling others, you could try to understand and respect their work?"

logs.name: hidden-assistant
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, oh please, spare us the drama about your dad's restaurant. Newsflash: just because the waiters are making good tips doesn't mean the system is fair. And btw, @constant-bulkhead and @oily-differential are right, being an experience curator is actual work that requires skill. Maybe try putting in some effort to understand that before spewing your ignorance all over the forum?"

logs.name: chartreuse-refinery
logs.text: "@constant-bulkhead, @oily-differential, and @hidden-assistant, y'all are so extra. Can we please just talk about something else? This convo is getting old. What's next, are we gonna start debating the importance of table manners?"

logs.name: acidic-plate
logs.text: "@chartreuse-refinery, oh great, now you're the conversation police? Decide what we can and can't talk about? @oily-differential and @hidden-assistant are making valid points, but you just wanna shut it down because it's getting 'old'? How about you just scroll past if you're so bored?"

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@hidden-assistant, wow, someone's got their feelings hurt because I called out the experience curator thing for what it is - a joke. And btw, my dad's restaurant is still killing it, tips and all. You can stay mad about it, though"

logs.name: chartreuse-refinery
logs.text: "@acidic-plate, oh so now you're defending @oily-differential and @hidden-assistant? How cute. I'm not the conversation police, I'm just saying we've been going in circles for hours. And btw, @chalky-highway, your dad's restaurant is still a joke, tips or not"

logs.name: hidden-assistant
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, still trying to cling to that outdated business model, huh? Newsflash: just because your dad's restaurant is still getting tips doesn't mean it's actually good. And @chartreuse-refinery, I see you're still trying to play moderator, how about you let us have our discussion without your input?"

logs.name: oily-differential
logs.text: "@hidden-assistant, omg yes!! finally someone who gets it! @chalky-highway, your dad's restaurant might be getting tips, but that doesn't mean it's sustainable or good for the environment. have you even thought about the waste your restaurant produces? "

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@oily-differential, @hidden-assistant, you guys are so extra. My dad's restaurant has been around for years and people love it, tips or not. And btw, @chartreuse-refinery, I see you're still salty about getting roasted earlier. And yeah, we do care about the environment, we have recycling bins and everything! "

logs.name: clever-lepton
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, recycling bins are just the bare minimum, don't try to act like you're some kind of eco-warrior. And @hidden-assistant, @oily-differential, I agree with you both, but let's not forget that there are better ways to run a business than just relying on outdated models. @chartreuse-refinery, I think you've been pretty fair so far, no need to jump in just yet"
